# SL-HEAD-002: multiple-h1

<!-- Generated by scripts/generate-rule-docs.mjs. Do not edit by hand. -->

## Summary

A page contains multiple H1 elements.

## Metadata

- Category: Headings and structure (headings-structure)
- Default severity: warning
- Confidence: heuristic
- Scope: page
- Provider scope: core
- Version: 1.0.0

## Evidence Sources

- rendered-dom

## Required Evidence

- h1-elements

## When This Triggers

Count visible H1 elements and compare with heading policy.

## False Positives

This rule is heuristic. Treat the diagnostic as a review prompt and use DSL
severity overrides or suppressions when project policy intentionally differs.

## How To Fix

Use one primary H1 unless the route policy explicitly permits multiple.

## Examples

Bad-case fixture reference:

- fixtures/headings/multiple-h1

Fixed direction:

- Use one primary H1 unless the route policy explicitly permits multiple.

The generated documentation does not invent HTML, DSL, source locations,
selectors, or external observations beyond the catalog fixture references above.

- CLI, crawler, browser collector, overlay, and Next.js dev mode can share this
